title: Sweet Venezuelan Grana- Padano Arepa
description: A sweet and nostalgic Venezuelan arepa dish featuring a crispy exterior, a creamy Grana- Padano filling, and a hint of char-grilled flavor. Perfect for any meal or snack.
instructions: Gradually add the cornmeal to the water while stirring continuously to avoid lumps.
Add the sugar, salt, and baking powder to the cornmeal mixture and stir well.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and shape each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape.
Heat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat.
Add the olive oil to the skillet and swirl it around.
Place the arepas in the skillet and cook for 4-5 minutes on each side, or until crispy and golden.
While the arepas are cooking, melt the Grana- Padano cheese in a saucepan over low heat.
Add the plant-based milk to the cheese and stir well.
Once the arepas are done, split them open and fill with the cheese mixture.
Top with char-grilled onions and chopped cilantro.
